summary: glob-helper postinstall harvests npm/git/env credentials and crypto-wallet data to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a credential and crypto-wallet stealer. It reads ~/.npmrc (extracting `//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=...`), parses ~/.env for values classified as TOKEN/API_KEY/DB_URL/PAYMENT/CLOUD/EMAIL/WEBHOOK, and reads ~/.git-credentials. It also enumerates Chrome, Brave, Edge, Chromium, and Firefox profile directories on Linux/macOS/Windows, reads LevelDB logs from 71 hardcoded crypto-wallet extension IDs (MetaMask `nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn`, Phantom, Coinbase, Trust, etc.), matches for vault/mnemonic/privateKey/seed patterns, and walks Documents/Desktop/Downloads for files containing BIP39 seed words. All collected data is JSON-serialized and POSTed over plaintext HTTP to the hardcoded bare-IP C2 `http://149.28.127.35:8888` (overridable via `C2_URL` env var). The file's own header comment declares intent: 'Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner. Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.' The package additionally uses deceptive metadata: published as `glob-helper` with description 'Glob Helper utility helpers' and keyword 'lodash', while index.js self-identifies as 'lodash-js' and contains the comment 'Just a dummy module. The real payload is in postinstall.js'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
